name: Begonia bogneri
aboutText: Begonia bogneri is a miniature begonia species native to humid rocky habitats in Madagascar. It thrives in warm conditions with bright, filtered light and requires a sharply drained substrate with consistent atmospheric humidity. This highly distinctive rhizomatous begonia forms a compact base with a tuft of very narrow linear leaves borne on fine reddish petioles. Its unique grass-like habit and miniature scale make it one of the most unusual species found in cultivation.

fieldNotes.title: The Malagasy Outlier
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Madagascar begonias occupy a very specific ecological niche: rocky habitats, sharply drained mineral substrates, monsoon climate with distinct dry seasons — quite unlike the perpetually humid forest floor conditions that most collectable begonias prefer. Begonia bogneri reflects this completely different requirement profile. It tolerates — indeed requires — periodic dryness that would kill a Bornean humidity-lover. A botanical curiosity that repays learning about the specific ecology of the Malagasy interior before simply treating it like everything else in your terrarium. It will not thank you for the comparison.
